category_title: Confident students with articulation-based Spoken Language Disorders
scenario_title: Articulation difficulty during vocabulary peer talk
minimal_context: The student is participating in a peer turn-and-talk activity about who inspires them following an interactive read-aloud of 'Rocket Writes a Story'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just finished reading 'Rocket Writes a Story'. Now it is time to turn and talk with your group. Who is someone that inspires you?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: My sister. She sires [sic] me to get my homework done. My sister sires me [sic].

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We just finished reading 'Rocket Writes a Story'. Now it is time to turn and talk with your group. Who is someone that inspires you?

STUDENT: I will start the conversation.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead.

STUDENT: I know my answer.

TUTOR: Tell us.

STUDENT: My sister. She sires [sic] me to get my homework done. My sister sires me [sic].

task.content: Using the explicitly taught vocabulary word 'inspires' during peer talk following an interactive read-aloud of 'Rocket Writes a Story', responding to the prompt 'Who is someone that inspires you?'
task.learning_goal: Consistently and accurately use novel words in context and expand on responses

context.environment: Kindergarten classroom during interactive read-aloud (IRA) sessions
context.social_setting: Peer conversations with planned opportunities to turn and talk
context.time_context: Following the explicit teaching of the vocabulary word before the read aloud

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When attempting to use the novel vocabulary word 'inspires' in conversation during peer talk
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Demonstrated difficulty with articulation; was unable to pronounce it correctly, substituting sounds by saying 'sires' instead of 'inspires'
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Comfortable using the word even though she was unable to pronounce it correctly
related_quotes: My sister. She sires [sic] me to get my homework done. My sister sires me [sic].

behavior_patterns.condition: When using explicitly taught vocabulary words in conversation
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Could not articulate certain sounds in words, but still took a leadership role and was the first to engage in peer talk
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Appeared to be confident in each of the observed conversations
related_quotes: I declared that I couldn’t sleep.
I saw a bird perch on the end of a tree branch.
